session_summary: A short session resuming the imputation-engine sensitivity screen (Amelia/EMB vs Bayes-proper data augmentation, todo/017), then pivoting to acquire and cite the Amelia (Honaker) and Tanner-Wong data-augmentation references. Marcus kicks it off with a detailed plan-of-record prompt, then catches a gap the AI missed (the engines are not Bayesianly proper in Schafer's sense even if sensitivity is not detected, so the manuscript should acknowledge it), and drives the litrev acquisition/citekey-fix loop.

coded_turns.quote:
Resume mi-spectral: SCREEN imputation-engine sensitivity (Bayes-proper DA vs EMB), then
  escalate ONLY if necessary.
coded_turns.note: Detailed resume prompt setting a DA-minimizing tiered screening design with decision rules, gotchas, and verification gates.

coded_turns.quote: they are not Bayesianly proper in the Shafer sense and may carry limitations (bootstraping discribed in little and rubin as approaximate bayesian, etc.). I'm thining we need to update the manuscript
coded_turns.note: Catches that not-sensitive does not equal proper; demands a manuscript limitation citing Schafer/Little-Rubin on approximate-Bayesian bootstrap.

coded_turns.quote: can you migrate the pdfs to google drive
coded_turns.note: Operational instruction to migrate the newly-added Amelia and Tanner-Wong PDFs into the litrev pipeline.
